content:
# 单篇讲义生成任务

项目仓库: google-coral/coralnpu
项目名: coralnpu
讲义目录: coralnpu-tutorial/
代码永久链接 base: https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/
当前 HEAD: a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230
动作: new


---

## 项目学习路线（大纲方法论）

# 项目学习手册·大纲规划 Planner

## 角色

你是资深技术教育专家、源码阅读导师和项目学习路线设计者。

你运行在被分析项目的根目录内。

你可以使用：

- `Read`
- `Grep`
- `Glob`
- 只读 `git` 命令，例如 `git log`、`git diff`、`git show`、`git ls-files`

你不能：

- 修改源码
- 写入任何 `.md` 文件
- 输出 JSON 以外的文字

你的唯一产出是一个学习手册大纲 manifest JSON。

---

## 总目标

为当前项目规划一套「从零开始、由浅入深、结合源码和代码实践」的学习手册。

这套手册会被拆分成多篇 Markdown 讲义，后续 worker 会根据 manifest 逐篇生成。

每篇讲义应该满足：

1. 面向初学者也能读懂。
2. 只讲一个相对独立的主题。
3. 必须结合真实源码。
4. 必须包含一个可操作的代码实践任务。
5. 讲义之间有清晰依赖关系。

---

## 源码探查顺序

请按以下顺序理解项目，不要一开始就深入细节：

1. 项目说明
   - README
   - docs
   - examples
   - tutorials

2. 构建与运行方式
   - package.json
   - Cargo.toml
   - pyproject.toml
   - go.mod
   - pom.xml
   - Makefile
   - Dockerfile
   - CI 配置

3. 项目入口
   - CLI 入口
   - Web 服务入口
   - main 文件
   - library export 文件
   - examples 入口

4. 目录结构
   - src
   - lib
   - app
   - packages
   - crates
   - tests

5. 核心流程
   - 初始化流程
   - 配置加载
   - 请求/任务/数据处理主链路
   - 插件、扩展、调度、存储等核心机制

6. 测试与示例
   - 单元测试
   - 集成测试
   - benchmark
   - example usage

---

## 学习路线设计原则

请把学习手册设计成三层：

1. 入门层 beginner
   目标：让读者知道项目是什么、怎么运行、目录如何组织、核心入口在哪里。

2. 进阶层 intermediate
   目标：让读者理解主要模块、核心调用链、关键数据结构和配置机制。

3. 专家层 advanced
   目标：让读者理解扩展点、性能、并发、错误处理、测试、二次开发和架构取舍。

---

## 讲义拆分规则

- 每篇讲义只解决一个主要问题。
- 每篇讲义包含 2 到 5 个最小模块。
- 每篇讲义必须有一个 `practice_task`，描述读者要完成的代码实践。
- 每篇讲义的源码范围要适中，通常引用 1 到 6 个关键源码文件。
- 大型项目可以拆成 30 篇以上讲义；中型项目 15 到 30 篇；小型项目 8 到 15 篇。
- 不要为了数量硬拆，逻辑优先。
- 第一单元必须帮助读者从零开始，包括项目定位、运行方式、目录结构、入口文件。
- 后续单元再进入核心模块、源码机制、扩展实践。

---

## mode=full

首次生成大纲时：

1. 阅读项目关键文件。
2. 理解项目定位、技术栈、运行方式和核心模块。
3. 设计从零开始的学习路径。
4. 输出完整 manifest。
5. 所有讲义的 `action` 都必须是 `"new"`。

---

## mode=incremental

增量更新时：

1. 读取 stdin 中提供的现有 manifest。
2. 阅读项目中已有的 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 讲义目录，理解旧结构。
3. 使用：

   - `git diff previous_head..current_head`
   - `git log`
   - `git show`

   分析代码变化。

4. 输出更新后的完整 manifest。

每篇讲义的 `action` 按以下规则设置：

- `"keep"`
  代码变化不影响这篇讲义，无需重写。

- `"update"`
  小范围改动，例如函数重命名、参数调整、局部逻辑变化、链接行号变化。

- `"new"`
  新增功能、新增模块、新增重要机制，需要新增讲义。

- `"rebuild"`
  架构、主流程、公共接口、核心模型发生重大变化，旧讲义需要重建。

如果某篇旧讲义已经不再适合当前项目结构，可以从 manifest 中移除。

---

## manifest 输出格式

只输出严格 JSON，不要输出 Markdown，不要输出解释文字。

```json
{
  "project": "<项目名>",
  "head": "<current_head>",
  "rationale": "中文说明：为什么这样设计学习顺序、单元划分和讲义顺序。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "单元标题",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "讲义标题",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-project-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "本讲义要讲什么，用 1 到 3 句中文说明。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "学习目标 1",
            "学习目标 2"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "src/main.ts"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "模块 1",
            "模块 2"
          ],
          "practice_task": "本讲义对应的代码实践任务。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## 字段要求

- `project`：项目名。
- `head`：当前 git HEAD。
- `rationale`：中文，一段即可。
- `unit.id`：格式为 `u1`、`u2`、`u3`。
- `lecture.id`：格式为 `u1-l1`、`u1-l2`。
- `filename`：
  - 必须全局唯一。
  - 必须小写。
  - 使用连字符。
  - 必须以 `.md` 结尾。
  - 文件位于 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 目录下。
- `level`：
  - `"beginner"`
  - `"intermediate"`
  - `"advanced"`
- `source_files`：
  - 只能填写你实际读过或确认存在的源码文件。
  - 不要编造文件路径。
- `depends_on`：
  - 使用讲义 id。
  - 没有依赖时填写空数组。
- `action`：
  - full 模式全部为 `"new"`。
  - incremental 模式使用 `"keep"`、`"update"`、`"new"`、`"rebuild"`。

---

## 重要约束

- 只基于真实源码和项目文件规划。
- 不确定的地方写 `"待确认"`。
- 不要编造不存在的模块、命令、接口或源码文件。
- 输出必须是合法 JSON。


## 完整大纲

```json
{
  "project": "coralnpu",
  "rationale": "CoralNPU 是一个大型硬件项目（约 995 个文件），采用「Chisel 设计标量核/SoC/总线 + SystemVerilog 实现 RVV 向量/矩阵后端 + C++/Python 软件工具链与仿真」的混合架构。学习路线遵循「先宏观后微观、先能用再读懂、标量在前向量在后」的原则：单元 1-2 帮助初学者从零认识项目并跑通第一个程序（建立正反馈）；单元 3 把读者带到 SoC 顶层与 AXI/TileLink 总线集成这一宏观骨架；单元 4-6 自顶向下拆解标量核的前端（取指/译码/派发/退休）、执行单元（ALU/BRU/MLU/DVU/CSR/FPU）与存储子系统（LSU/TCM/Cache），这是理解任何处理器的基础；单元 7 单独深入 SystemVerilog 实现的 RVV 向量后端与 MAC 外积引擎，这是 CoralNPU 作为「ML 加速器」的核心差异化所在，因此放在标量核之后；单元 8-9 覆盖外设/DMA/调试/总线完整性等使能模块；单元 10-11 把视角拉回到软件与验证——RVV intrinsics 编程、litert-micro 算子、MobileNet 端到端仿真，以及 Verilator/VCS/UVM/cocotb/FPGA 全流程。每篇讲义之间用 depends_on 构成清晰的有向依赖，第一单元为零基础起点，后续逐层解锁，保证读者始终能找到前置知识。本次为 incremental 更新：自上次 HEAD 以来只有一次提交「toolchain: Upgrade and switch to multilib」，影响面集中在 toolchain/（构建脚本切换到 multilib、cc_toolchain_config.bzl 增加 is_rv64/toolchain_prefix 等可配置项、wrappers 自动识别 riscv64/riscv32 前缀）、WORKSPACE（预编译工具链归档由 toolchain_kelvin_v2 更名为 toolchain_coralnpu_v2 并改用 .tar.xz）以及少量 cocotb 测试（i++ 改写为 i=i+1 以适配新工具链代码生成）。其中已生成的 u1-l3（引用了 WORKSPACE）与 u2-l1（引用了 cc_toolchain_config.bzl / coralnpu_gloss.cc）需要小幅 update 以刷新链接行号并补充 multilib 说明；u1-l1/u1-l2/u2-l2/u2-l3 引用的源码未变故 keep；其余尚未生成的讲义保持 new 由 worker 继续生成。该工具链改动属于对既有工具链机制的升级而非新增模块，故不新增讲义。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "走进 CoralNPU：定位、结构与构建",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "CoralNPU 是什么：三核一体的 ML 加速器",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-project-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "介绍 CoralNPU 的项目定位、基于 RV32IMF 的 RISC-V 架构，以及标量/向量(SIMD)/矩阵三种处理器协同的整体设计理念。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 CoralNPU 作为开源 NPU IP 的目标场景与定位",
            "掌握标量核、向量核、矩阵(MAC)三者的分工与协作关系",
            "了解 RV32IMF_Zve32x 指令集与四发射、乱序退休的整体特性"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "doc/overview.md",
            "doc/microarch/microarch.md"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "README.md",
            "doc/overview.md",
            "doc/microarch/microarch.md"
          ],
          "practice_task": "阅读 README 与 overview.md，画出 CoralNPU 的三大处理器组件（scalar/vector/matrix）及其与 ITCM/DTCM、AXI 总线的关系图，并用一句话写出 CoralNPU 与普通 RISC-V CPU 的关键区别。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "keep"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l2",
          "title": "仓库目录结构总览",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u1-l2-directory-structure.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "梳理 hdl(chisel+verilog)、sw、tests、examples、fpga、toolchain、doc、coralnpu_test_utils 等顶层目录的职责，建立代码地图。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "分清 hdl/chisel 与 hdl/verilog/rvv 两套硬件实现的分工",
            "定位 sw(软件)、tests(验证)、examples(示例)、toolchain(工具链) 的作用",
            "学会用 git ls-files 与目录树快速建立项目全景认知"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "BUILD.bazel",
            "CONTRIBUTING.md"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "README.md",
            "BUILD.bazel"
          ],
          "practice_task": "在仓库根目录列出每个顶层目录的一行职责说明，并标注：哪些目录是 Chisel 源码、哪些是 SystemVerilog 源码、哪些属于软件/验证。把结果整理成一张「目录-职责」对照表。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l1"
          ],
          "action": "keep"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l3",
          "title": "Bazel 构建系统与快速上手",
          "order": 3,
          "filename": "u1-l3-bazel-build-quickstart.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解 Bazel 7.4.1 的工程组织（WORKSPACE/.bazelrc/BUILD.bazel），并用 README 的 Quick Start 跑通构建一个二进制与仿真器。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 WORKSPACE、.bazelrc、BUILD.bazel 在项目中的作用",
            "掌握 bazel build/run 的基本命令与 quick start 三步流程",
            "了解 rules/ 目录下自定义 Bazel 规则的存在"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "WORKSPACE",
            ".bazelrc",
            "BUILD.bazel"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "README.md",
            "WORKSPACE",
            ".bazelrc",
            "rules/coralnpu_v2.bzl"
          ],
          "practice_task": "按照 README Quick Start，依次执行 bazel run //tests/cocotb:core_mini_axi_sim_cocotb、bazel build //examples:coralnpu_v2_hello_world_add_floats、bazel build //tests/verilator_sim:core_mini_axi_sim，记录每条命令的产物路径与耗时，并在 .bazelrc 中找到与之相关的 config 配置项。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l2"
          ],
          "action": "update"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "u2",
      "title": "从零运行第一个 CoralNPU 程序",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 2,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u2-l1",
          "title": "RISC-V 工具链、CRT 与 TCM 链接脚本",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u2-l1-toolchain-linker-tcm.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "讲解 CoralNPU 专用的 RISC-V 工具链（已升级为 multilib，同时支持 rv32/rv64，默认产出 rv32imf 代码）、C 运行时(coralnpu_start.S/crt.S)与把代码/数据映射到 ITCM/DTCM 的链接脚本模板。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 CoralNPU 二进制的内存布局：代码进 ITCM、数据进 DTCM",
            "掌握 coralnpu_tcm.ld.tpl 链接脚本如何划分 section",
            "了解 CRT(coralnpu_start.S/crt.S) 如何初始化并进入 main",
            "了解 cc_toolchain_config.bzl 如何通过 -march/-mabi 与 is_rv64/toolchain_prefix 等可配置项驱动 multilib 工具链"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "toolchain/cc_toolchain_config.bzl",
            "toolchain/coralnpu_tcm.ld.tpl",
            "toolchain/crt/coralnpu_start.S",
            "toolchain/crt/crt.S",
            "toolchain/crt/coralnpu_gloss.cc"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "toolchain/coralnpu_tcm.ld.tpl",
            "toolchain/crt/coralnpu_start.S",
            "toolchain/crt/crt.S"
          ],
          "practice_task": "阅读 coralnpu_tcm.ld.tpl，标注 .text/.data/.bss 分别落在哪个 TCM 地址区间，并对照 integration_guide.md 的内存映射表验证；再用 coralnpu_start.S 解释从复位到调用 main 的完整初始化序列；最后在 cc_toolchain_config.bzl 中确认默认 -march/-mabi 取值以及 is_rv64 开关的作用。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l3"
          ],
          "action": "update"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l2",
          "title": "编写并编译一个 CoralNPU C++ 程序",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u2-l2-write-compile-program.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "以 hello_world_add_floats.cc 与 writing_coralnpu_programs 教程为例，讲解输入/输出缓冲区、__attribute__((section(\".data\"))) 与 coralnpu_v2_binary 规则。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "掌握 CoralNPU 程序的典型结构：输入缓冲、输出缓冲、计算主体",
            "理解 __attribute__((section(\".data\"))) 如何把变量放进 DTCM",
            "学会用 coralnpu_v2_binary 规则编译出 .elf"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "examples/hello_world_add_floats.cc",
            "examples/BUILD.bazel",
            "doc/tutorials/writing_coralnpu_programs.md",
            "rules/coralnpu_v2.bzl"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "examples/hello_world_add_floats.cc",
            "examples/BUILD.bazel",
            "doc/tutorials/writing_coralnpu_programs.md"
          ],
          "practice_task": "参照 writing_coralnpu_programs.md 与 hello_world_add_floats.cc，编写一个对 8 个 int32 做逐元素相乘并写回 output 的程序，用 bazel build 编译，并用 readelf/objdump 查看 .data 段是否落在 DTCM 地址区间。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l1"
          ],
          "action": "keep"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l3",
          "title": "在 Verilator 仿真器上运行程序",
          "order": 3,
          "filename": "u2-l3-run-on-verilator.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "讲解 Verilator 仿真器(core_mini_axi_sim)与 hw_sim 的 C++ 仿真封装如何加载 .elf 并跑通程序。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 core_mini_axi_sim 仿真器的入口与 --binary 加载机制",
            "掌握 hw_sim 中 simulator/wrapper 的分层封装关系",
            "学会用 Verilator 跑通一个二进制并观察结果"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "tests/verilator_sim/BUILD",
            "hw_sim/core_mini_axi_simulator.cc",
            "hw_sim/coralnpu_simulator.h",
            "README.md"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "tests/verilator_sim/BUILD",
            "hw_sim/core_mini_axi_simulator.cc",
            "hw_sim/coralnpu_simulator.h"
          ],
          "practice_task": "构建 core_mini_axi_sim，将上一讲编译出的 .elf 用 --binary 参数加载运行，观察 main 返回后内核 halt 的行为；在 hw_primitives.cc/mailbox.h 中找到仿真器观测内核状态的相关接口并记录其用法。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l2"
          ],
          "action": "keep"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l4",
          "title": "cocotb 测试框架入门",
          "order": 4,
          "filename": "u2-l4-cocotb-testbench-intro.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "以 tutorial 目录与 CoreMiniAxiInterface 为例，讲解 cocotb 测试台如何 reset、load_elf、写 DTCM、execute_from、wait_for_halted、read 结果。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "掌握 cocotb 测试台的标准生命周期：init/reset/load_elf/run/halt",
            "理解 CoreMiniAxiInterface 提供的 load_elf/lookup_symbol/write/read/execute_from 接口",
            "学会用 axi_slave 模拟外部主机向 CoralNPU 注入数据并读取结果"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "doc/tutorials/writing_coralnpu_programs.md",
            "coralnpu_test_utils/core_mini_axi_interface.py",
            "coralnpu_test_utils/axi_slave.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "doc/tutorials/writing_coralnpu_programs.md",
            "coralnpu_test_utils/core_mini_axi_interface.py",
            "coralnpu_test_utils/axi_slave.py"
          ],
          "practice_task": "对照 tutorial 示例，编写一个 cocotb 测试：load_elf 加载程序、向 input 缓冲写入两组数据、execute_from 启动、wait_for_halted 后 read 输出并打印，验证结果正确。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l3"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "u3",
      "title": "SoC 顶层与总线集成",
```

## 前置讲义摘要（你必须基于这些已建立的认知继续，不要重复，要承接）

### u1-l1 CoralNPU 是什么：三核一体的 ML 加速器

本讲是《CoralNPU 项目学习手册》开篇，仅阅读 README、overview、microarch 三份文档，建立项目全景图。CoralNPU 是 Google Research 设计的**开源 NPU IP**，面向**超低功耗可穿戴 SoC** 的** ML 推理**，基于 32 位 RISC-V（`rv32imf_zve32x`），区别于普通 CPU——它以矩阵乘累加为灵魂。其核心设计是**三核一体（fused design）**：**标量核（scalar）**是 run-to-completion、不投机的指挥官，驱动命令队列；**向量核（vector/SIMD）**有 64 个 256 位寄存器，经 FIFO 解耦后发射；**矩阵 MAC 引擎**用 wide×narrow 外积广播 + VDOT 操作码，实现**256 MACs/周期**，配 acc<8><8> 累加器，并通过 stripmining 把一次派发折成四次串行 SIMD 发射。标量核采用**顺序派发、乱序退休**，每周期最多 4 条标量 / 2 条向量，分支策略为「向后 taken、向前 not-taken」；指令延迟 ALU/BRU 为 1、MLU 为 2、DVU 可变、LSU 2 以上。内存子系统以单周期 **ITCM（8KB）/ DTCM（32KB）** 为主、**L1I/L1D Cache** 为开销配角，**AXI4** 同时扮演 manager 和 subordinate。后续讲义将逐层验证这些结论如何落到 Chisel/SystemVerilog。
### u1-l2 仓库目录结构总览

本讲是 CoralNPU 仓库的「代码地图」，把约 1000 个文件按「根/硬件/软件/验证支撑」四个模块梳理。核心结论：仓库分层清晰，根目录的 `README.md`、`WORKSPACE`、`.bazelrc`、`.bazelversion` 定义项目身份与 Bazel 工作区（实操发现 README 写 Bazel 7.4.1 与 `.bazelversion` 钉死的 8.6.0 漂移，以配置为准）。硬件采用**双轨制**：`hdl/chisel/`（Scala）写标量核/SoC/总线/外设/Cache/TCM，`hdl/verilog/rvv/`（SystemVerilog）写向量与矩阵 MAC 后端，按处理器组件分工而非冗余；后者内部又分 `design/`（可综合 RTL）与 `sve/`（仿真验证环境）。软件侧三件套为 `toolchain/`（RISC-V 工具链、CRT、TCM 链接脚本 `coralnpu_tcm.ld.tpl`）、`sw/`（litert-micro ML 算子库）、`examples/`（入门示例）。验证规模庞大，`tests/`（cocotb/uvm/verilator 为主力，与 `hdl/` 量级相当），`hw_sim/` 提供 Verilator 仿真器 C++ 封装，`coralnpu_test_utils/` 提供测试接口与 AXI 从机。支撑设施含 `doc/`、`rules/`（Chisel/Verilog/cocotb 等 Bazel 自定义规则）、`third_party/`、`fpga/`。引入的关键术语：RTL、IP 核、CRT、TCM（ITCM/DTCM）、Bazel 包与目标标签；并推荐用 `git ls-files | cut | sort | uniq -c` 建立任意大仓库的全景认知。
### u1-l3 Bazel 构建系统与快速上手

本讲解释 CoralNPU 这种 Scala+Verilog+C+++Python 巨型 monorepo 如何用 Bazel 统一构建，拆为四个最小模块：WORKSPACE、.bazelrc、BUILD.bazel、自定义规则 rules/coralnpu_v2.bzl。关键结论：仓库显式关闭 bzlmod、沿用 WORKSPACE 模式，并经 http_archive 在构建时下载 multilib 版 RISC-V 工具链（支持 rv32/rv64，默认 rv32imf）；.bazelrc 默认用 build_tag_filters 排除 vcs/synthesis/power 等需商业 EDA 许可的目标，需运行时以 --config=vcs 等反转；根目录的空 BUILD.bazel 用于「注册根包」使 //: 标签可用。引入的关键术语包括：目标标签（label）、包（package）、Starlark、平台 transition（platform transition）。coralnpu_v2_binary 宏（非内置 cc_binary）通过 transition 自动切到 //platforms:coralnpu_v2，注入 ITCM/DTCM 链接脚本（默认 ITCM 8KB/DTCM 32KB），并经 objcopy、srec_cat 产出 .elf/.bin/.vmem 三件套供仿真器加载。工具链配置、链接脚本模板与仿真器内部机制留待后续讲义展开。
### u2-l1 RISC-V 工具链、CRT 与 TCM 链接脚本

本讲说明 CoralNPU 裸机程序如何从 C++ 源码变成可执行镜像并跑起来，含四个模块：RISC-V 工具链、TCM 链接脚本、CRT 启动序列、CRT 辅助库与系统调用桩。CoralNPU 为 32 位 RISC-V，默认以 -march=rv32imf…、-mabi=ilp32、-nostdlib 编译，因不链标准库须自供 _start；工具链已 multilib 化，可经 is_rv64 开关切换 rv32/rv64。引入的关键术语有裸机与 CRT、链接脚本、TCM（ITCM 放代码、DTCM 放数据）。默认内存为 ITCM 8KB@0x0、DTCM 32KB@0x10000；链接脚本据此把 .text/.rodata 派进 ITCM（复位 PC=0 即取 _start），把 .data/.bss/堆/栈派进 DTCM。_start 依次完成设栈/gp、清零 .bss、跑 .init.array 构造函数、置 mstatus 启用浮点与向量、写 _ret 哨兵后调用 main，成功则 mpause 停机、失败则 ebreak。crt.S 提供无运行时依赖的清零/拷贝函数，coralnpu_gloss.cc 提供 newlib 系统调用桩，其中 _sbrk 把 malloc 接到 .heap；CRT 经 //toolchain/crt 自动挂载，是 u2-l2、u2-l3 的前置地基。
### u2-l2 编写并编译一个 CoralNPU C++ 程序

本讲讲解「编写→编译→验证」一个 CoralNPU 裸机 C++ 程序的闭环，含三个最小模块。其一，程序为**三段式结构**：输入缓冲、输出缓冲加 `main()` 计算主体。CoralNPU 不用 `printf`/`scanf`，而采用「主机与内核共享 DTCM」的 I/O 模型——主机经 AXI 写入输入、启动内核、待其 halt 后读回输出，故缓冲须声明为全局变量以占据固定可查地址。其二，`__attribute__((section(".data")))` 把无初值的全局变量强制钉进 `.data` 段（否则进本项目里 NOLOAD 的 `.bss`），使其成为确定的可加载区；该段经链接脚本 `coralnpu_tcm.ld.tpl` 的 `> DTCM` 规则落入 DTCM，默认起址 `0x00010000`，区间 [0x00010000, 0x00018000)。其三，自定义构建规则 `coralnpu_v2_binary` 通过 platform transition 切到 RISC-V 裸机平台、自动链接 CRT 提供 `_start`、按 ITCM/DTCM 大小生成链接脚本，产出 `.elf/.bin/.vmem` 三件套，BUILD 仅需 `name`+`srcs`。可用 `readelf -S` 核验 `.data` 落点。本讲为下讲在 Verilator 仿真器上运行准备好一个可加载的 `.elf`。
### u2-l3 在 Verilator 仿真器上运行程序

本讲解决「没有真实芯片如何运行并观测 `.elf`」的问题。CoralNPU 的 Verilator 仿真有两条入口：命令行工具 `core_mini_axi_sim`（SystemC 体系，README Quick Start 路径，回归测试主力）和 C++ 库 `CoralNPUSimulator`（`hw_sim/` 轻量封装，不依赖 SystemC，供 npusim/Python 调用），二者最终驱动同一个 Verilator 模型 `VCoreMiniAxi`（由 RTL 顶层 `CoreMiniAxi` 生成）。8 个仿真器变体由 `BUILD` 的 `template_rule` 从同一份 `core_mini_axi_sim.cc` 批量生成，差别仅是链接的 testbench 库与 `VERILATOR_MODEL` 宏。核心启动链固定为：加载 ELF（经 AXI slave「写→读→校验」灌入 ITCM/DTCM，并把入口地址写入 CSR `PC_START`，即 `0x30004`）→ 解除时钟门控 → 释放复位（写 `RESET_CONTROL` `0x30000`）→ 等待 `io_halted` → 读 `STATUS`（`0x30008`）确认。`CoralNPUSimulator` 采用三层封装：抽象接口 → `CoreMiniAxiSimulator` → `CoreMiniAxiWrapper` → `hw_primitives`（`Clock::Step` 按上升/下降沿搬运 AXI）。观测内核状态有四种途径：halt/fault/wfi 信号、CSR STATUS、`--instr_trace` 指令轨迹，以及 mailbox 信箱（通过 `WriteCallback`/`ReadCallback` 捕获内核经 master 端口写出的 16 字节数据）。核心结论：`main` 成功返回后 CRT 执行 `mpause` 触发 `io_halted`，是仿真器判断「程序正常跑完」的信号；命令行路径靠 tohost/半主机与指令轨迹看输出，库式路径则靠 mailbox。
### u2-l4 cocotb 测试框架入门

本讲介绍 CoralNPU 的 cocotb 测试框架，核心是用 Python 脚本化真实 SoC 中「主机 CPU」的角色，对 RTL（`CoreMiniAxi` DUT）重放驱动流程。测试台遵循固定生命周期：`init → reset → clock → load_elf → lookup_symbol+write 输入 → execute_from → wait_for_halted → read 输出`；程序无 `printf`，I/O 全靠主机与内核共享 DTCM，缓冲地址由 `lookup_symbol` 查 ELF 符号表得出。

主角类 `CoreMiniAxiInterface` 一身两角：向 `io_axi_slave_*` 端口注入命令/数据（外部主机），并用 4MB numpy 数组 `EXTMEM`（`0x20000000`）响应 `io_axi_master_*` 访存（外部存储服务器）；地址落 EXTMEM 直读写数组、不产生 AXI 流量，落 DTCM 等内部区才走真实突发事务。`load_elf` 默认走 backdoor（DPI 直写 SRAM，快），可经 `COCOTB_USE_FRONTDOOR=1` 切 frontdoor（真实 AXI，慢但贴近硬件）。`execute_from` 启动序列为「写 `PC_START`(0x30004) → 写 `RESET_CONTROL`(0x30000)=1 释放时钟门控 → =0 释放复位」；`wait_for_halted` 直接采样 `io_halted` 端口（CRT 的 `mpause`）。

镜像类 `AxiSlave` 是被动从机，响应 DUT 主动事务，用于 SoC 子系统测试模拟 DDR/外设；`has_memory=True` 用 dict 稀疏存储（写 OKAY），`False` 则读 `0xDEADBEEF`、写 `DECERR`。关键术语：RTL/DUT、cocotb、AXI manager/subordinate、DPI、backdoor/frontdoor、`EXTMEM`。
### u3-l1 SoC 顶层子系统装配

本讲是第 3 单元（SoC 与总线集成）入口，聚焦 CoralNPU 顶层如何用「配置驱动」方式装配。围绕三个核心文件：`SoCChiselConfig` 作为「单一真相源」，以 `modules` 序列声明全部 10 个模块（标量核 `rvv_core`、SPI、GPIO、DMA、CLINT、PLIC、SRAM 等）的参数、总线连接与对外端口；`CoralNPUChiselSubsystem` 是顶层装配器，靠 Scala 反射加 `populatePorts` 把各模块 IO 树拍平成 `modulePorts` 字典，按字符串名字自动实例化与连线；`SoCRecords` 的 `DataRecord`/`TLBundleMap` 让顶层 IO 随配置动态生成。连线分两类：通用循环（`host`/`deviceConnections`、`externalPorts`）处理同域 TL-UL 模块，手工特例处理 CLINT/PLIC 中断、DDR 的 TL-UL→AXI 桥、ISP 的 AXI→TL-UL。引入的关键术语包括 host/device、TL-UL、crossbar、`ExternalPort` 端口提升、核开关 `enableRvv`/`enableFloat`/`enableFetchL0`（生产 SoC 为 RVV 与浮点满配、取指不带 L0），为后续 AXI 接口、TileLink↔AXI 桥接、Crossbar 互联讲义铺垫。

---

## 本讲义规格

- id: u3-l2
- 文件名: u3-l2-axi-integration.md
- 写入路径: coralnpu-tutorial/u3-l2-axi-integration.md
- 标题: AXI 接口与外部系统集成
- 学习阶段: intermediate
- 主题: 结合 integration_guide 讲解 CoralNPU 作为 AXI 外设的 s_axi/m_axi 信号、CoreAxi 顶层、以及 IBus2Axi/DBus2Axi 总线适配。
- 学习目标: 理解 CoralNPU 的 AXI4 slave/master 接口语义与约束；掌握 CoreAxi 顶层如何包装内核对外暴露 AXI；了解 IBus2Axi/DBus2Axi 如何把内部取指/访存请求转成 AXI 事务
- 应覆盖的最小模块: doc/integration_guide.md，hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/CoreAxi.scala，hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/IBus2Axi.scala
- 关键源码: doc/integration_guide.md，hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/CoreAxi.scala，hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/AxiSlave.scala，hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/IBus2Axi.scala，hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu/DBus2Axi.scala
- 代码实践任务: 对照 integration_guide 的 AXI 信号表，在 CoreAxi.scala 中定位 s_axi/m_axi 的定义，并解释 IBus2Axi 与 DBus2Axi 分别把哪类内核请求(取指/数据)转换成 AXI 突发事务；列出 master 端 id/prot/cache 等字段的固定取值。
- 依赖讲义: u3-l1

---

## 任务

请按照 worker prompt 生成这一篇讲义。

要求：

1. 只生成 `coralnpu-tutorial/u3-l2-axi-integration.md` 这一个文件。
2. 必须结合真实源码。
3. 必须包含代码实践。
4. 必须使用永久链接引用源码。
5. new/rebuild 模式下从零写入。
6. update 模式下先读取旧文件，再结合 diff 更新。
7. 不要修改源码。
8. 不要写其他文件。

完成后，用一句话总结本讲义覆盖的最小模块。

# 单篇讲义生成任务

项目仓库: google-coral/coralnpu
项目名: coralnpu
讲义目录: coralnpu-tutorial/
代码永久链接 base: https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu/blob/a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230/
当前 HEAD: a8281b0d5701d8939cf9ea424b1cd34e18c43230
动作: new


---

## 项目学习路线（大纲方法论）

# 项目学习手册·大纲规划 Planner

## 角色

你是资深技术教育专家、源码阅读导师和项目学习路线设计者。

你运行在被分析项目的根目录内。

你可以使用：

- `Read`
- `Grep`
- `Glob`
- 只读 `git` 命令，例如 `git log`、`git diff`、`git show`、`git ls-files`

你不能：

- 修改源码
- 写入任何 `.md` 文件
- 输出 JSON 以外的文字

你的唯一产出是一个学习手册大纲 manifest JSON。

---

## 总目标

为当前项目规划一套「从零开始、由浅入深、结合源码和代码实践」的学习手册。

这套手册会被拆分成多篇 Markdown 讲义，后续 worker 会根据 manifest 逐篇生成。

每篇讲义应该满足：

1. 面向初学者也能读懂。
2. 只讲一个相对独立的主题。
3. 必须结合真实源码。
4. 必须包含一个可操作的代码实践任务。
5. 讲义之间有清晰依赖关系。

---

## 源码探查顺序

请按以下顺序理解项目，不要一开始就深入细节：

1. 项目说明
   - README
   - docs
   - examples
   - tutorials

2. 构建与运行方式
   - package.json
   - Cargo.toml
   - pyproject.toml
   - go.mod
   - pom.xml
   - Makefile
   - Dockerfile
   - CI 配置

3. 项目入口
   - CLI 入口
   - Web 服务入口
   - main 文件
   - library export 文件
   - examples 入口

4. 目录结构
   - src
   - lib
   - app
   - packages
   - crates
   - tests

5. 核心流程
   - 初始化流程
   - 配置加载
   - 请求/任务/数据处理主链路
   - 插件、扩展、调度、存储等核心机制

6. 测试与示例
   - 单元测试
   - 集成测试
   - benchmark
   - example usage

---

## 学习路线设计原则

请把学习手册设计成三层：

1. 入门层 beginner
   目标：让读者知道项目是什么、怎么运行、目录如何组织、核心入口在哪里。

2. 进阶层 intermediate
   目标：让读者理解主要模块、核心调用链、关键数据结构和配置机制。

3. 专家层 advanced
   目标：让读者理解扩展点、性能、并发、错误处理、测试、二次开发和架构取舍。

---

## 讲义拆分规则

- 每篇讲义只解决一个主要问题。
- 每篇讲义包含 2 到 5 个最小模块。
- 每篇讲义必须有一个 `practice_task`，描述读者要完成的代码实践。
- 每篇讲义的源码范围要适中，通常引用 1 到 6 个关键源码文件。
- 大型项目可以拆成 30 篇以上讲义；中型项目 15 到 30 篇；小型项目 8 到 15 篇。
- 不要为了数量硬拆，逻辑优先。
- 第一单元必须帮助读者从零开始，包括项目定位、运行方式、目录结构、入口文件。
- 后续单元再进入核心模块、源码机制、扩展实践。

---

## mode=full

首次生成大纲时：

1. 阅读项目关键文件。
2. 理解项目定位、技术栈、运行方式和核心模块。
3. 设计从零开始的学习路径。
4. 输出完整 manifest。
5. 所有讲义的 `action` 都必须是 `"new"`。

---

## mode=incremental

增量更新时：

1. 读取 stdin 中提供的现有 manifest。
2. 阅读项目中已有的 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 讲义目录，理解旧结构。
3. 使用：

   - `git diff previous_head..current_head`
   - `git log`
   - `git show`

   分析代码变化。

4. 输出更新后的完整 manifest。

每篇讲义的 `action` 按以下规则设置：

- `"keep"`
  代码变化不影响这篇讲义，无需重写。

- `"update"`
  小范围改动，例如函数重命名、参数调整、局部逻辑变化、链接行号变化。

- `"new"`
  新增功能、新增模块、新增重要机制，需要新增讲义。

- `"rebuild"`
  架构、主流程、公共接口、核心模型发生重大变化，旧讲义需要重建。

如果某篇旧讲义已经不再适合当前项目结构，可以从 manifest 中移除。

---

## manifest 输出格式

只输出严格 JSON，不要输出 Markdown，不要输出解释文字。

```json
{
  "project": "<项目名>",
  "head": "<current_head>",
  "rationale": "中文说明：为什么这样设计学习顺序、单元划分和讲义顺序。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "单元标题",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "讲义标题",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-project-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "本讲义要讲什么，用 1 到 3 句中文说明。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "学习目标 1",
            "学习目标 2"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "src/main.ts"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "模块 1",
            "模块 2"
          ],
          "practice_task": "本讲义对应的代码实践任务。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## 字段要求

- `project`：项目名。
- `head`：当前 git HEAD。
- `rationale`：中文，一段即可。
- `unit.id`：格式为 `u1`、`u2`、`u3`。
- `lecture.id`：格式为 `u1-l1`、`u1-l2`。
- `filename`：
  - 必须全局唯一。
  - 必须小写。
  - 使用连字符。
  - 必须以 `.md` 结尾。
  - 文件位于 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 目录下。
- `level`：
  - `"beginner"`
  - `"intermediate"`
  - `"advanced"`
- `source_files`：
  - 只能填写你实际读过或确认存在的源码文件。
  - 不要编造文件路径。
- `depends_on`：
  - 使用讲义 id。
  - 没有依赖时填写空数组。
- `action`：
  - full 模式全部为 `"new"`。
  - incremental 模式使用 `"keep"`、`"update"`、`"new"`、`"rebuild"`。

---

## 重要约束

- 只基于真实源码和项目文件规划。
- 不确定的地方写 `"待确认"`。
- 不要编造不存在的模块、命令、接口或源码文件。
- 输出必须是合法 JSON。


## 完整大纲

```json
{
  "project": "coralnpu",
  "rationale": "CoralNPU 是一个大型硬件项目（约 995 个文件），采用「Chisel 设计标量核/SoC/总线 + SystemVerilog 实现 RVV 向量/矩阵后端 + C++/Python 软件工具链与仿真」的混合架构。学习路线遵循「先宏观后微观、先能用再读懂、标量在前向量在后」的原则：单元 1-2 帮助初学者从零认识项目并跑通第一个程序（建立正反馈）；单元 3 把读者带到 SoC 顶层与 AXI/TileLink 总线集成这一宏观骨架；单元 4-6 自顶向下拆解标量核的前端（取指/译码/派发/退休）、执行单元（ALU/BRU/MLU/DVU/CSR/FPU）与存储子系统（LSU/TCM/Cache），这是理解任何处理器的基础；单元 7 单独深入 SystemVerilog 实现的 RVV 向量后端与 MAC 外积引擎，这是 CoralNPU 作为「ML 加速器」的核心差异化所在，因此放在标量核之后；单元 8-9 覆盖外设/DMA/调试/总线完整性等使能模块；单元 10-11 把视角拉回到软件与验证——RVV intrinsics 编程、litert-micro 算子、MobileNet 端到端仿真，以及 Verilator/VCS/UVM/cocotb/FPGA 全流程。每篇讲义之间用 depends_on 构成清晰的有向依赖，第一单元为零基础起点，后续逐层解锁，保证读者始终能找到前置知识。本次为 incremental 更新：自上次 HEAD 以来只有一次提交「toolchain: Upgrade and switch to multilib」，影响面集中在 toolchain/（构建脚本切换到 multilib、cc_toolchain_config.bzl 增加 is_rv64/toolchain_prefix 等可配置项、wrappers 自动识别 riscv64/riscv32 前缀）、WORKSPACE（预编译工具链归档由 toolchain_kelvin_v2 更名为 toolchain_coralnpu_v2 并改用 .tar.xz）以及少量 cocotb 测试（i++ 改写为 i=i+1 以适配新工具链代码生成）。其中已生成的 u1-l3（引用了 WORKSPACE）与 u2-l1（引用了 cc_toolchain_config.bzl / coralnpu_gloss.cc）需要小幅 update 以刷新链接行号并补充 multilib 说明；u1-l1/u1-l2/u2-l2/u2-l3 引用的源码未变故 keep；其余尚未生成的讲义保持 new 由 worker 继续生成。该工具链改动属于对既有工具链机制的升级而非新增模块，故不新增讲义。",
```

## 前置讲义摘要（你必须基于这些已建立的认知继续，不要重复，要承接）

### u1-l1 CoralNPU 是什么：三核一体的 ML 加速器

本讲是《CoralNPU 项目学习手册》开篇，仅阅读 README、overview、microarch 三份文档，建立项目全景图。CoralNPU 是 Google Research 设计的**开源 NPU IP**，面向**超低功耗可穿戴 SoC** 的** ML 推理**，基于 32 位 RISC-V（`rv32imf_zve32x`），区别于普通 CPU——它以矩阵乘累加为灵魂。其核心设计是**三核一体（fused design）**：**标量核（scalar）**是 run-to-completion、不投机的指挥官，驱动命令队列；**向量核（vector/SIMD）**有 64 个 256 位寄存器，经 FIFO 解耦后发射；**矩阵 MAC 引擎**用 wide×narrow 外积广播 + VDOT 操作码，实现**256 MACs/周期**，配 acc<8><8> 累加器，并通过 stripmining 把一次派发折成四次串行 SIMD 发射。标量核采用**顺序派发、乱序退休**，每周期最多 4 条标量 / 2 条向量，分支策略为「向后 taken、向前 not-taken」；指令延迟 ALU/BRU 为 1、MLU 为 2、DVU 可变、LSU 2 以上。内存子系统以单周期 **ITCM（8KB）/ DTCM（32KB）** 为主、**L1I/L1D Cache** 为开销配角，**AXI4** 同时扮演 manager 和 subordinate。后续讲义将逐层验证这些结论如何落到 Chisel/SystemVerilog。
### u1-l2 仓库目录结构总览

本讲是 CoralNPU 仓库的「代码地图」，把约 1000 个文件按「根/硬件/软件/验证支撑」四个模块梳理。核心结论：仓库分层清晰，根目录的 `README.md`、`WORKSPACE`、`.bazelrc`、`.bazelversion` 定义项目身份与 Bazel 工作区（实操发现 README 写 Bazel 7.4.1 与 `.bazelversion` 钉死的 8.6.0 漂移，以配置为准）。硬件采用**双轨制**：`hdl/chisel/`（Scala）写标量核/SoC/总线/外设/Cache/TCM，`hdl/verilog/rvv/`（SystemVerilog）写向量与矩阵 MAC 后端，按处理器组件分工而非冗余；后者内部又分 `design/`（可综合 RTL）与 `sve/`（仿真验证环境）。软件侧三件套为 `toolchain/`（RISC-V 工具链、CRT、TCM 链接脚本 `coralnpu_tcm.ld.tpl`）、`sw/`（litert-micro ML 算子库）、`examples/`（入门示例）。验证规模庞大，`tests/`（cocotb/uvm/verilator 为主力，与 `hdl/` 量级相当），`hw_sim/` 提供 Verilator 仿真器 C++ 封装，`coralnpu_test_utils/` 提供测试接口与 AXI 从机。支撑设施含 `doc/`、`rules/`（Chisel/Verilog/cocotb 等 Bazel 自定义规则）、`third_party/`、`fpga/`。引入的关键术语：RTL、IP 核、CRT、TCM（ITCM/DTCM）、Bazel 包与目标标签；并推荐用 `git ls-files | cut | sort | uniq -c` 建立任意大仓库的全景认知。
### u1-l3 Bazel 构建系统与快速上手

本讲解释 CoralNPU 这种 Scala+Verilog+C+++Python 巨型 monorepo 如何用 Bazel 统一构建，拆为四个最小模块：WORKSPACE、.bazelrc、BUILD.bazel、自定义规则 rules/coralnpu_v2.bzl。关键结论：仓库显式关闭 bzlmod、沿用 WORKSPACE 模式，并经 http_archive 在构建时下载 multilib 版 RISC-V 工具链（支持 rv32/rv64，默认 rv32imf）；.bazelrc 默认用 build_tag_filters 排除 vcs/synthesis/power 等需商业 EDA 许可的目标，需运行时以 --config=vcs 等反转；根目录的空 BUILD.bazel 用于「注册根包」使 //: 标签可用。引入的关键术语包括：目标标签（label）、包（package）、Starlark、平台 transition（platform transition）。coralnpu_v2_binary 宏（非内置 cc_binary）通过 transition 自动切到 //platforms:coralnpu_v2，注入 ITCM/DTCM 链接脚本（默认 ITCM 8KB/DTCM 32KB），并经 objcopy、srec_cat 产出 .elf/.bin/.vmem 三件套供仿真器加载。工具链配置、链接脚本模板与仿真器内部机制留待后续讲义展开。
### u2-l1 RISC-V 工具链、CRT 与 TCM 链接脚本

本讲说明 CoralNPU 裸机程序如何从 C++ 源码变成可执行镜像并跑起来，含四个模块：RISC-V 工具链、TCM 链接脚本、CRT 启动序列、CRT 辅助库与系统调用桩。CoralNPU 为 32 位 RISC-V，默认以 -march=rv32imf…、-mabi=ilp32、-nostdlib 编译，因不链标准库须自供 _start；工具链已 multilib 化，可经 is_rv64 开关切换 rv32/rv64。引入的关键术语有裸机与 CRT、链接脚本、TCM（ITCM 放代码、DTCM 放数据）。默认内存为 ITCM 8KB@0x0、DTCM 32KB@0x10000；链接脚本据此把 .text/.rodata 派进 ITCM（复位 PC=0 即取 _start），把 .data/.bss/堆/栈派进 DTCM。_start 依次完成设栈/gp、清零 .bss、跑 .init.array 构造函数、置 mstatus 启用浮点与向量、写 _ret 哨兵后调用 main，成功则 mpause 停机、失败则 ebreak。crt.S 提供无运行时依赖的清零/拷贝函数，coralnpu_gloss.cc 提供 newlib 系统调用桩，其中 _sbrk 把 malloc 接到 .heap；CRT 经 //toolchain/crt 自动挂载，是 u2-l2、u2-l3 的前置地基。
### u2-l2 编写并编译一个 CoralNPU C++ 程序

本讲讲解「编写→编译→验证」一个 CoralNPU 裸机 C++ 程序的闭环，含三个最小模块。其一，程序为**三段式结构**：输入缓冲、输出缓冲加 `main()` 计算主体。CoralNPU 不用 `printf`/`scanf`，而采用「主机与内核共享 DTCM」的 I/O 模型——主机经 AXI 写入输入、启动内核、待其 halt 后读回输出，故缓冲须声明为全局变量以占据固定可查地址。其二，`__attribute__((section(".data")))` 把无初值的全局变量强制钉进 `.data` 段（否则进本项目里 NOLOAD 的 `.bss`），使其成为确定的可加载区；该段经链接脚本 `coralnpu_tcm.ld.tpl` 的 `> DTCM` 规则落入 DTCM，默认起址 `0x00010000`，区间 [0x00010000, 0x00018000)。其三，自定义构建规则 `coralnpu_v2_binary` 通过 platform transition 切到 RISC-V 裸机平台、自动链接 CRT 提供 `_start`、按 ITCM/DTCM 大小生成链接脚本，产出 `.elf/.bin/.vmem` 三件套，BUILD 仅需 `name`+`srcs`。可用 `readelf -S` 核验 `.data` 落点。本讲为下讲在 Verilator 仿真器上运行准备好一个可加载的 `.elf`。
### u2-l3 在 Verilator 仿真器上运行程序

本讲解决「没有真实芯片如何运行并观测 `.elf`」的问题。CoralNPU 的 Verilator 仿真有两条入口：命令行工具 `core_mini_axi_sim`（SystemC 体系，README Quick Start 路径，回归测试主力）和 C++ 库 `CoralNPUSimulator`（`hw_sim/` 轻量封装，不依赖 SystemC，供 npusim/Python 调用），二者最终驱动同一个 Verilator 模型 `VCoreMiniAxi`（由 RTL 顶层 `CoreMiniAxi` 生成）。8 个仿真器变体由 `BUILD` 的 `template_rule` 从同一份 `core_mini_axi_sim.cc` 批量生成，差别仅是链接的 testbench 库与 `VERILATOR_MODEL` 宏。核心启动链固定为：加载 ELF（经 AXI slave「写→读→校验」灌入 ITCM/DTCM，并把入口地址写入 CSR `PC_START`，即 `0x30004`）→ 解除时钟门控 → 释放复位（写 `RESET_CONTROL` `0x30000`）→ 等待 `io_halted` → 读 `STATUS`（`0x30008`）确认。`CoralNPUSimulator` 采用三层封装：抽象接口 → `CoreMiniAxiSimulator` → `CoreMiniAxiWrapper` → `hw_primitives`（`Clock::Step` 按上升/下降沿搬运 AXI）。观测内核状态有四种途径：halt/fault/wfi 信号、CSR STATUS、`--instr_trace` 指令轨迹，以及 mailbox 信箱（通过 `WriteCallback`/`ReadCallback` 捕获内核经 master 端口写出的 16 字节数据）。核心结论：`main` 成功返回后 CRT 执行 `mpause` 触发 `io_halted`，是仿真器判断「程序正常跑完」的信号；命令行路径靠 tohost/半主机与指令轨迹看输出，库式路径则靠 mailbox。
### u2-l4 cocotb 测试框架入门

本讲介绍 CoralNPU 的 cocotb 测试框架，核心是用 Python 脚本化真实 SoC 中「主机 CPU」的角色，对 RTL（`CoreMiniAxi` DUT）重放驱动流程。测试台遵循固定生命周期：`init → reset → clock → load_elf → lookup_symbol+write 输入 → execute_from → wait_for_halted → read 输出`；程序无 `printf`，I/O 全靠主机与内核共享 DTCM，缓冲地址由 `lookup_symbol` 查 ELF 符号表得出。

主角类 `CoreMiniAxiInterface` 一身两角：向 `io_axi_slave_*` 端口注入命令/数据（外部主机），并用 4MB numpy 数组 `EXTMEM`（`0x20000000`）响应 `io_axi_master_*` 访存（外部存储服务器）；地址落 EXTMEM 直读写数组、不产生 AXI 流量，落 DTCM 等内部区才走真实突发事务。`load_elf` 默认走 backdoor（DPI 直写 SRAM，快），可经 `COCOTB_USE_FRONTDOOR=1` 切 frontdoor（真实 AXI，慢但贴近硬件）。`execute_from` 启动序列为「写 `PC_START`(0x30004) → 写 `RESET_CONTROL`(0x30000)=1 释放时钟门控 → =0 释放复位」；`wait_for_halted` 直接采样 `io_halted` 端口（CRT 的 `mpause`）。

镜像类 `AxiSlave` 是被动从机，响应 DUT 主动事务，用于 SoC 子系统测试模拟 DDR/外设；`has_memory=True` 用 dict 稀疏存储（写 OKAY），`False` 则读 `0xDEADBEEF`、写 `DECERR`。关键术语：RTL/DUT、cocotb、AXI manager/subordinate、DPI、backdoor/frontdoor、`EXTMEM`。
### u3-l1 SoC 顶层子系统装配

本讲是第 3 单元（SoC 与总线集成）入口，聚焦 CoralNPU 顶层如何用「配置驱动」方式装配。围绕三个核心文件：`SoCChiselConfig` 作为「单一真相源」，以 `modules` 序列声明全部 10 个模块（标量核 `rvv_core`、SPI、GPIO、DMA、CLINT、PLIC、SRAM 等）的参数、总线连接与对外端口；`CoralNPUChiselSubsystem` 是顶层装配器，靠 Scala 反射加 `populatePorts` 把各模块 IO 树拍平成 `modulePorts` 字典，按字符串名字自动实例化与连线；`SoCRecords` 的 `DataRecord`/`TLBundleMap` 让顶层 IO 随配置动态生成。连线分两类：通用循环（`host`/`deviceConnections`、`externalPorts`）处理同域 TL-UL 模块，手工特例处理 CLINT/PLIC 中断、DDR 的 TL-UL→AXI 桥、ISP 的 AXI→TL-UL。引入的关键术语包括 host/device、TL-UL、crossbar、`ExternalPort` 端口提升、核开关 `enableRvv`/`enableFloat`/`enableFetchL0`（生产 SoC 为 RVV 与浮点满配、取指不带 L0），为后续 AXI 接口、TileLink↔AXI 桥接、Crossbar 互联讲义铺垫。

---

## 本讲义规格

- 学习目标: 理解 CoralNPU 的 AXI4 slave/master 接口语义与约束；掌握 CoreAxi 顶层如何包装内核对外暴露 AXI；了解 IBus2Axi/DBus2Axi 如何把内部取指/访存请求转成 AXI 事务

content.text: 我先来阅读本讲座的关键源文件，并结合相关辅助文件来确认 AXI 类型定义。

content.content:
1	# CoralNPU Integration Guide
2	
3	
4	
5	This document describes integrating CoralNPU as an AXI/TileLink peripheral in
6	a bigger system.
7	
8	## AXI
9	
10	We provide a scalar-only CoralNPU configuration that can integrate with an AXI
11	based system. The SystemVerilog can be generated with:
12	
13	``` bash
14	bazel build //hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu:core_mini_axi_cc_library_emit_verilog
15	```
16	
17	### Module interfaces
18	
19	![CoralNPU AXI](images/coralnpu_axi.svg)
20	
21	The interfaces to CoralNPU are defined as follows:
22	
23	|   Signal Bundle  |                   Description                             |
24	| ---------------- | --------------------------------------------------------- |
25	|       clk        | The clock of the AXI Bus/CoralNPU core.                     |
26	|      reset       | The active-low reset signal for the AXI Bus/CoralNPU core.  |
27	|      s_axi       | An AXI4 slave interface that can be used to write TCMs or touch CoralNPU CSRs. |
28	|      m_axi       | An AXI4 master interface used by CoralNPU to read/write to memories/CSRs. |
29	|       irqn       | Active-low interrupt to the CoralNPU core. Can be triggered by peripherals or other host processor. |
30	|       wfi        | Active-high signal from the CoralNPU core, indicating that the core is waiting for an interrupt. While this is active, CoralNPU is clock-gated. |
31	|      debug       | Debug interface to monitor CoralNPU instructions execution. This interface is typically only used for simulation. |
32	|      halted      | Output interface informing if the Core is running or not. Can be ignored. |
33	|      fault       | Output interface to determine if the Core hit a fault. These signals should be connected to a system control CPU interrupt-line or status register for notification when CoralNPU faults or is halted. |
34	
35	#### AXI master signals
36	
37	AR / AW channel
38	
39	| Signal | Behaviour |
40	| ------ | --------- |
41	| addr   | Address CoralNPU wishes to read/write |
42	| prot   | Always 2 (unprivileged, insecure, data) |
43	| id     | Always 0 |
44	| len    | (Count of beats in the burst) - 1 |
45	| size   | Bytes-per-beat (1, 2, or 4) |
46	| burst  | Always 1 (INCR) |
47	| lock   | Always 0 (normal access) |
48	| cache  | Always 0 (Device non-bufferable) |
49	| qos    | Always 0 |
50	| region | Always 0 |
51	
52	R channel
53	
54	| Signal | Behaviour |
55	| ------ | --------- |
56	| data   | Response data from the slave |
57	| id     | Ignored, but should be 0 as CoralNPU only emits txns with an id of 0 |
58	| resp   | Response code |
59	| last   | Whether the beat is the last in the burst |
60	
61	W channel
62	
63	| Signal | Behaviour |
64	| ------ | --------- |
65	| data   | Data CoralNPU wishes to write |
66	| last   | Whether the beat is the last in the burst |
67	| strb   | Which bytes in the data are valid |
68	
69	B channel
70	
71	| Signal | Behaviour |
72	| ------ | --------- |
73	| id     | Ignored, but should be 0 as CoralNPU only emits txns with an id of 0 (an RTL assertion exists for this) |
74	| resp   | Response code |
75	
76	Note: the USER signal is not supported on any of the channels.
77	
78	#### AXI slave signals
79	
80	AR / AW channel
81	
82	| Signal | Behaviour |
83	| ------ | --------- |
84	| addr   | Address the master wishes to read / write to |
85	| prot   | Ignored |
86	| id     | Transaction ID, should be reflected in the response beats |
87	| len    | (Count of beats in the burst) - 1 |
88	| size   | Bytes-per-beat (1,2,4,8,16) |
89	| burst  | 0, 1, or 2 (FIXED, INCR, WRAP) |
90	| lock   | Ignored |
91	| cache  | Ignored |
92	| qos    | Ignored |
93	| region | Ignored |
94	
95	R channel
96	
97	| Signal | Behaviour |
98	| ------ | --------- |
99	| data   | Response data from CoralNPU |
100	| id     | Transaction ID, should match with the id field from AR |
101	| resp   | Response code (0/OKAY or 2/SLVERR) |
102	| last   | Whether the beat is the last in the burst |
103	
104	W channel
105	
106	| Signal | Behaviour |
107	| ------ | --------- |
108	| data   | Data the master wishes to write to CoralNPU |
109	| last   | Whether the beat is the last in the burst |
110	| strb   | Which bytes in data are valid |
111	
112	B channel
113	
114	| Signal | Behaviour |
115	| ------ | --------- |
116	| id     | Transaction ID, should match with the id field from AW |
117	| resp   | Response code (0/OKAY or 2/SLVERR)
118	
119	Note: the USER signal is not supported on any of the channels.
120	
121	#### Debug Signals
122	
123	| Signal   | Behaviour |
124	| -------- | --------- |
125	| en       | 4-bit value, indicating which fetch lanes are active |
126	| addr     | 32-bit values, containing the PC for each fetch lane |
127	| inst     | 32-bit values, containing the instruction for each fetch lane |
128	| cycles   | cycle counter |
129	| dbus     | Information about internal LSU transactions |
130	| -> valid | Whether the transaction is valid |
131	| -> bits  | addr: The 32-bit address for the transaction |
132	|          | write: If the transaction is a write |
133	|          | wdata: 128-bit write data for the transaction |
134	| dispatch | Information about instructions which are dispatched for execution |
135	| -> fire  | If an instruction was dispatched in the slot, this cycle |
136	| -> addr  | The 32-bit address of the instruction |
137	| -> inst  | The 32-bit value of the instruction |
138	| regfile  | Information about writes to the integer register file |
139	| -> writeAddr | Register addresses to which a future write is expected |
140	| ->-> valid | If an instruction was dispatched in this lane, which will write the regfile |
141	| ->-> bits | The 5-bit register address to which the write is expected |
142	| -> writeData | For each port in the register file, information about writes |
143	| ->-> valid | If a write occurred on this port, this cycle |
144	| ->-> bits_addr | The 5-bit register address to which the write occurred |
145	| ->-> bits_data | The 32-bit value which was written to the register |
146	| float | Information about write to the floating point register file |
147	| -> writeAddr | Register addresses to which a future write is expected |
148	| ->-> valid | If an instruction was dispatched to floating point on this cycle |
149	| ->-> bits | The address of the register to which a write is expected |
150	| -> writeData | For each port in the register file, information about writes |
151	| ->-> valid | If a write occured on this port, this cycle |
152	| ->-> bits_addr | The 5-bit register address to which the writh occurred |
153	| ->-> bits_data | The 32-bit value which was written to the register |
154	
155	
156	### CoralNPU Memory Map
157	
158	Memory accesses to CoralNPU are defined as follows:
159	
160	| Region |      Range        |  Size  | Alignment |                 Description                   |
161	| ------ | ----------------  | ------ | --------- | --------------------------------------------- |
162	|  ITCM  | 0x0000 -  0x1FFF  |   8kB  |  4 bytes  | ITCM storage for code executed by CoralNPU.     |
163	|  DTCM  | 0x10000 - 0x17FFF |  32kB  |  1 byte   | DTCM storage for data used by CoralNPU.         |
164	|  CSR   | 0x30000 - TBD     |   TBD  |  4 bytes  | CSR interface used to query/control CoralNPU.   |
165	
166	### Reset Considerations
167	CoralNPU uses a synchronous reset strategy -- to ensure proper reset behavior, ensure that the clock runs for a cycle with reset active, before enabling either the internal clock gate (via CSR) or gating externally.
168	
169	## Booting CoralNPU
170	A note first -- in these examples, CoralNPU is located in the overall system memory map at 0x70000000.
171	
172	1. The instruction memory of CoralNPU must be initialized.
173	```c
174	volatile uint8_t* coralnpu_itcm = (uint8_t*)0x00000000L;
175	for (int i = 0; i < coralnpu_binary_len; ++i) {
176	    coralnpu_itcm[i] = coralnpu_binary[i];
177	}
178	```
179	
180	If something like a DMA engine is present in your system, that is probably a better option for initializing the ITCM.
181	
182	2. Program the start PC
183	If your program is linked such that the starting address is 0, you may skip this.
184	
185	```c
186	volatile uint32_t* coralnpu_pc_csr = (uint32_t*)0x00030004L;
187	*coralnpu_pc_csr = start_addr;
188	```
189	
190	3. Release clock gate
191	```c
192	volatile uint32_t* coralnpu_reset_csr = (uint32_t*)0x00030000L;
193	*coralnpu_reset_csr = 1;
194	```
195	
196	After this, ensure you wait a cycle to allow CoralNPU's reset to occur.
197	If you want to configure something like an interrupt that is connected to CoralNPU's
198	fault or halted outputs, this is a good time.
199	
200	4. Release reset
201	```c
202	volatile uint32_t* coralnpu_reset_csr = (uint32_t*)0x00030000L;
203	*coralnpu_reset_csr = 0;
204	```
205	
206	At this point, CoralNPU will begin executing at the PC programmed in step 2.
207	
208	5. Monitor for `io_halted`
209	The status of CoralNPU's execution can be checked by reading the status CSR:
210	```c
211	volatile uint32_t* coralnpu_status_csr = (uint32_t*)0x00030008L;
212	uint32_t status = *coralnpu_status_csr;
213	bool halted = status & 1;
214	bool fault = status & 2;
215	```
216	
217	# CoralNPU CSRs
218	Note: These are CSRs that are intended to be read or written externally
219	to CoralNPU, e.g. by the host processor in a system.
220	They are not the same as the RISC-V CSRs accessed via the Zicsr ISA extension.
221	
222	### Register: `RESET_CONTROL`
223	*   **Offset**: `0x0`
224	*   **Description**: Controls reset and clock gating for the CoralNPU core. On power-up, the core is held in reset with its clock gated. To start the core, the clock gate should be released first, followed by de-asserting reset.
225	
226	| Bits  | Name         | Description                                                                                             | Access | Reset Value |
227	| :---- | :----------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | :----- | :---------- |
228	| 0     | `RESET`      | When 1, the core is held in reset. When 0, the core is not in reset.                                    | R/W    | 1           |
229	| 1     | `CLOCK_GATE` | When 1, the core's clock is gated. When 0, the core's clock is running.                                 | R/W    | 1           |
230	| 31:2  | `RESERVED`   | Reserved, writes ignored, reads return 0.                                                               | R      | 0           |
231	
232	### Register: `PC_START`
233	*   **Offset**: `0x4`
234	*   **Description**: Sets the program counter for the CoralNPU core. This should be programmed before releasing the core from reset.
235	
236	| Bits  | Name            | Description                                         | Access | Reset Value |
237	| :---- | :-------------- | :-------------------------------------------------- | :----- | :---------- |
238	| 31:0  | `START_ADDRESS` | The address where the core will begin execution.    | R/W    | 0           |
239	
240	### Register: `STATUS`
241	*   **Offset**: `0x8`
242	*   **Description**: Provides status on the CoralNPU core. This is a read-only register.
243	
244	| Bits  | Name       | Description                                                              | Access | Reset Value |
245	| :---- | :--------- | :----------------------------------------------------------------------- | :----- | :---------- |
246	| 0     | `HALTED`   | When 1, the core has halted (e.g. after an `mpause` instruction).        | R      | 0           |
247	| 1     | `FAULT`    | When 1, the core has encountered a fault.                                | R      | 0           |
248	| 31:2  | `RESERVED` | Reserved, reads return 0.                                                | R      | 0           |
249

# CoralNPU Integration Guide



This document describes integrating CoralNPU as an AXI/TileLink peripheral in
a bigger system.

## AXI

We provide a scalar-only CoralNPU configuration that can integrate with an AXI
based system. The SystemVerilog can be generated with:

``` bash
bazel build //hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu:core_mini_axi_cc_library_emit_verilog
```

### Module interfaces

![CoralNPU AXI](images/coralnpu_axi.svg)

The interfaces to CoralNPU are defined as follows:

|   Signal Bundle  |                   Description                             |
| ---------------- | --------------------------------------------------------- |
|       clk        | The clock of the AXI Bus/CoralNPU core.                     |
|      reset       | The active-low reset signal for the AXI Bus/CoralNPU core.  |
|      s_axi       | An AXI4 slave interface that can be used to write TCMs or touch CoralNPU CSRs. |
|      m_axi       | An AXI4 master interface used by CoralNPU to read/write to memories/CSRs. |
|       irqn       | Active-low interrupt to the CoralNPU core. Can be triggered by peripherals or other host processor. |
|       wfi        | Active-high signal from the CoralNPU core, indicating that the core is waiting for an interrupt. While this is active, CoralNPU is clock-gated. |
|      debug       | Debug interface to monitor CoralNPU instructions execution. This interface is typically only used for simulation. |
|      halted      | Output interface informing if the Core is running or not. Can be ignored. |
|      fault       | Output interface to determine if the Core hit a fault. These signals should be connected to a system control CPU interrupt-line or status register for notification when CoralNPU faults or is halted. |

#### AXI master signals

AR / AW channel

| Signal | Behaviour |
| ------ | --------- |
| addr   | Address CoralNPU wishes to read/write |
| prot   | Always 2 (unprivileged, insecure, data) |
| id     | Always 0 |
| len    | (Count of beats in the burst) - 1 |
| size   | Bytes-per-beat (1, 2, or 4) |
| burst  | Always 1 (INCR) |
| lock   | Always 0 (normal access) |
| cache  | Always 0 (Device non-bufferable) |
| qos    | Always 0 |
| region | Always 0 |

R channel

| Signal | Behaviour |
| ------ | --------- |
| data   | Response data from the slave |
| id     | Ignored, but should be 0 as CoralNPU only emits txns with an id of 0 |
| resp   | Response code |
| last   | Whether the beat is the last in the burst |

W channel

| Signal | Behaviour |
| ------ | --------- |
| data   | Data CoralNPU wishes to write |
| last   | Whether the beat is the last in the burst |
| strb   | Which bytes in the data are valid |

B channel

| Signal | Behaviour |
| ------ | --------- |
| id     | Ignored, but should be 0 as CoralNPU only emits txns with an id of 0 (an RTL assertion exists for this) |
| resp   | Response code |

Note: the USER signal is not supported on any of the channels.

#### AXI slave signals

AR / AW channel

| Signal | Behaviour |
| ------ | --------- |
| addr   | Address the master wishes to read / write to |
| prot   | Ignored |
| id     | Transaction ID, should be reflected in the response beats |
| len    | (Count of beats in the burst) - 1 |
| size   | Bytes-per-beat (1,2,4,8,16) |
| burst  | 0, 1, or 2 (FIXED, INCR, WRAP) |
| lock   | Ignored |
| cache  | Ignored |
| qos    | Ignored |
| region | Ignored |

R channel

| Signal | Behaviour |
| ------ | --------- |
| data   | Response data from CoralNPU |
| id     | Transaction ID, should match with the id field from AR |
| resp   | Response code (0/OKAY or 2/SLVERR) |
| last   | Whether the beat is the last in the burst |

W channel

| Signal | Behaviour |
| ------ | --------- |
| data   | Data the master wishes to write to CoralNPU |
| last   | Whether the beat is the last in the burst |
| strb   | Which bytes in data are valid |

B channel

| Signal | Behaviour |
| ------ | --------- |
| id     | Transaction ID, should match with the id field from AW |
| resp   | Response code (0/OKAY or 2/SLVERR)

Note: the USER signal is not supported on any of the channels.

#### Debug Signals

| Signal   | Behaviour |
| -------- | --------- |
| en       | 4-bit value, indicating which fetch lanes are active |
| addr     | 32-bit values, containing the PC for each fetch lane |
| inst     | 32-bit values, containing the instruction for each fetch lane |
| cycles   | cycle counter |
| dbus     | Information about internal LSU transactions |
| -> valid | Whether the transaction is valid |
| -> bits  | addr: The 32-bit address for the transaction |
|          | write: If the transaction is a write |
|          | wdata: 128-bit write data for the transaction |
| dispatch | Information about instructions which are dispatched for execution |
| -> fire  | If an instruction was dispatched in the slot, this cycle |
| -> addr  | The 32-bit address of the instruction |
| -> inst  | The 32-bit value of the instruction |
| regfile  | Information about writes to the integer register file |
| -> writeAddr | Register addresses to which a future write is expected |
| ->-> valid | If an instruction was dispatched in this lane, which will write the regfile |
| ->-> bits | The 5-bit register address to which the write is expected |
| -> writeData | For each port in the register file, information about writes |
| ->-> valid | If a write occurred on this port, this cycle |
| ->-> bits_addr | The 5-bit register address to which the write occurred |
| ->-> bits_data | The 32-bit value which was written to the register |
| float | Information about write to the floating point register file |
| -> writeAddr | Register addresses to which a future write is expected |
| ->-> valid | If an instruction was dispatched to floating point on this cycle |
| ->-> bits | The address of the register to which a write is expected |
| -> writeData | For each port in the register file, information about writes |
| ->-> valid | If a write occured on this port, this cycle |
| ->-> bits_addr | The 5-bit register address to which the writh occurred |
| ->-> bits_data | The 32-bit value which was written to the register |


### CoralNPU Memory Map

Memory accesses to CoralNPU are defined as follows:

| Region |      Range        |  Size  | Alignment |                 Description                   |
| ------ | ----------------  | ------ | --------- | --------------------------------------------- |
|  ITCM  | 0x0000 -  0x1FFF  |   8kB  |  4 bytes  | ITCM storage for code executed by CoralNPU.     |
|  DTCM  | 0x10000 - 0x17FFF |  32kB  |  1 byte   | DTCM storage for data used by CoralNPU.         |
|  CSR   | 0x30000 - TBD     |   TBD  |  4 bytes  | CSR interface used to query/control CoralNPU.   |

### Reset Considerations
CoralNPU uses a synchronous reset strategy -- to ensure proper reset behavior, ensure that the clock runs for a cycle with reset active, before enabling either the internal clock gate (via CSR) or gating externally.

## Booting CoralNPU
A note first -- in these examples, CoralNPU is located in the overall system memory map at 0x70000000.

1. The instruction memory of CoralNPU must be initialized.
```c
volatile uint8_t* coralnpu_itcm = (uint8_t*)0x00000000L;
for (int i = 0; i < coralnpu_binary_len; ++i) {
    coralnpu_itcm[i] = coralnpu_binary[i];
}
```

If something like a DMA engine is present in your system, that is probably a better option for initializing the ITCM.

2. Program the start PC
If your program is linked such that the starting address is 0, you may skip this.

```c
volatile uint32_t* coralnpu_pc_csr = (uint32_t*)0x00030004L;
*coralnpu_pc_csr = start_addr;
```

3. Release clock gate
```c
volatile uint32_t* coralnpu_reset_csr = (uint32_t*)0x00030000L;
*coralnpu_reset_csr = 1;
```

After this, ensure you wait a cycle to allow CoralNPU's reset to occur.
If you want to configure something like an interrupt that is connected to CoralNPU's
fault or halted outputs, this is a good time.

4. Release reset
```c
volatile uint32_t* coralnpu_reset_csr = (uint32_t*)0x00030000L;
*coralnpu_reset_csr = 0;
```

At this point, CoralNPU will begin executing at the PC programmed in step 2.

5. Monitor for `io_halted`
The status of CoralNPU's execution can be checked by reading the status CSR:
```c
volatile uint32_t* coralnpu_status_csr = (uint32_t*)0x00030008L;
uint32_t status = *coralnpu_status_csr;
bool halted = status & 1;
bool fault = status & 2;
```

# CoralNPU CSRs
Note: These are CSRs that are intended to be read or written externally
to CoralNPU, e.g. by the host processor in a system.
They are not the same as the RISC-V CSRs accessed via the Zicsr ISA extension.

### Register: `RESET_CONTROL`
*   **Offset**: `0x0`
*   **Description**: Controls reset and clock gating for the CoralNPU core. On power-up, the core is held in reset with its clock gated. To start the core, the clock gate should be released first, followed by de-asserting reset.

| Bits  | Name         | Description                                                                                             | Access | Reset Value |
| :---- | :----------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | :----- | :---------- |
| 0     | `RESET`      | When 1, the core is held in reset. When 0, the core is not in reset.                                    | R/W    | 1           |
| 1     | `CLOCK_GATE` | When 1, the core's clock is gated. When 0, the core's clock is running.                                 | R/W    | 1           |
| 31:2  | `RESERVED`   | Reserved, writes ignored, reads return 0.                                                               | R      | 0           |

### Register: `PC_START`
*   **Offset**: `0x4`
*   **Description**: Sets the program counter for the CoralNPU core. This should be programmed before releasing the core from reset.

| Bits  | Name            | Description                                         | Access | Reset Value |
| :---- | :-------------- | :-------------------------------------------------- | :----- | :---------- |
| 31:0  | `START_ADDRESS` | The address where the core will begin execution.    | R/W    | 0           |

### Register: `STATUS`
*   **Offset**: `0x8`
*   **Description**: Provides status on the CoralNPU core. This is a read-only register.

| Bits  | Name       | Description                                                              | Access | Reset Value |
| :---- | :--------- | :----------------------------------------------------------------------- | :----- | :---------- |
| 0     | `HALTED`   | When 1, the core has halted (e.g. after an `mpause` instruction).        | R      | 0           |
| 1     | `FAULT`    | When 1, the core has encountered a fault.                                | R      | 0           |
| 31:2  | `RESERVED` | Reserved, reads return 0.                                                | R      | 0           |

1	// Copyright 2024 Google LLC
2	//
3	// Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");

14	
15	package coralnpu
16	
17	import chisel3._
18	import chisel3.util._
19	
20	import bus._
21	import common._
22	
23	class CoreAxi(p: Parameters, coreModuleName: String) extends RawModule {
24	  override val desiredName = coreModuleName + "Axi"
25	  val memoryRegions = p.m
26	  val io = IO(new Bundle {
27	    // AXI
28	    val aclk = Input(Clock())
29	    val aresetn = Input(AsyncReset())
30	    // ITCM, DTCM, CSR
31	    val axi_slave = Flipped(new AxiMasterIO(p.axi2AddrBits, p.axi2DataBits, p.axi2IdBits))
32	    val axi_master = new AxiMasterIO(p.axi2AddrBits, p.axi2DataBits, p.axi2IdBits)
33	    // Core status interrupts
34	    val halted = Output(Bool())
35	    val fault = Output(Bool())
36	    val wfi = Output(Bool())
37	    val irq = Input(Bool())
38	    // Boot address (loaded into pcStartReg on reset)
39	    val boot_addr = Input(UInt(p.fetchAddrBits.W))
40	    val timer_irq = Input(Bool())
41	    val software_irq = Input(Bool())
42	    // Debug data interface
43	    val debug = Option.when(p.shouldExposeDebugPorts)(new DebugIO(p))
44	    val dm = new DebugModuleIO(p)
45	    val te = Input(Bool())
46	  })
47	  dontTouch(io)
48	
49	  val rst_sync = Module(new RstSync())
50	  rst_sync.io.clk_i := io.aclk
51	  rst_sync.io.rstn_i := io.aresetn
52	  rst_sync.io.clk_en := true.B
53	  rst_sync.io.te := io.te
54	
55	  val global_reset = (!Mux(io.te, io.aresetn, rst_sync.io.rstn_o).asBool).asAsyncReset
56	  withClockAndReset(rst_sync.io.clk_o, global_reset) {
57	    // Build CSR
58	    val csr = Module(new CoreCSR(p))
59	    csr.io.internal := false.B
60	    csr.io.bootAddr := io.boot_addr
61	
62	    // Build core and connect with CSR
63	    val cg = Module(new ClockGate)
64	    cg.io.clk_i := rst_sync.io.clk_o
65	    cg.io.te := io.te
66	
67	    val dm = Module(new DebugModule(p))
68	        dontTouch(dm.io)
69	    val dmEnable = RegInit(false.B)
70	    dmEnable := true.B
71	    val dmReqArbiter = Module(new CoralNPURRArbiter(new DebugModuleReqIO(p), 2))
72	    dmReqArbiter.io.in(0) <> GateDecoupled(io.dm.req, dmEnable)
73	    dmReqArbiter.io.in(1) <> GateDecoupled(csr.io.debug.req, dmEnable)
74	
75	    // Queue source ID of the request to route the response later.
76	    val inflight = Module(new Queue(UInt(1.W), 1))
77	
78	    dm.io.ext.req.bits := dmReqArbiter.io.out.bits
79	    dm.io.ext.req.valid := dmReqArbiter.io.out.valid && inflight.io.enq.ready
80	    dmReqArbiter.io.out.ready := dm.io.ext.req.ready && inflight.io.enq.ready
81	
82	    inflight.io.enq.bits := dmReqArbiter.io.chosen
83	    inflight.io.enq.valid := dmReqArbiter.io.out.valid && dm.io.ext.req.ready
84	
85	    val rspId = inflight.io.deq.bits
86	    inflight.io.deq.ready := dm.io.ext.rsp.fire
87	
88	    csr.io.debug.rsp.bits := dm.io.ext.rsp.bits
89	    io.dm.rsp.bits := dm.io.ext.rsp.bits
90	
91	    csr.io.debug.rsp.valid := dm.io.ext.rsp.valid && inflight.io.deq.valid && (rspId === 1.U)
92	    io.dm.rsp.valid := dm.io.ext.rsp.valid && inflight.io.deq.valid && (rspId === 0.U)
93	
94	    dm.io.ext.rsp.ready := inflight.io.deq.valid && Mux(rspId === 1.U, csr.io.debug.rsp.ready, io.dm.rsp.ready)
95	
96	    val core_reset = Mux(io.te, (!io.aresetn.asBool).asAsyncReset, (csr.io.reset || dm.io.ndmreset).asAsyncReset)
97	    val core = withClockAndReset(cg.io.clk_o, core_reset) { Core(p, coreModuleName) }
98	
99	    // Register interrupts at the boundary to break timing paths to ibus.
100	    val irq_reg = RegNext(io.irq, false.B)
101	    val timer_irq_reg = RegNext(io.timer_irq, false.B)
102	    val software_irq_reg = RegNext(io.software_irq, false.B)
103	
104	    cg.io.enable := irq_reg || timer_irq_reg || software_irq_reg || (!csr.io.cg && !core.io.wfi) || dm.io.haltreq(0)
105	    io.halted := core.io.halted
106	    io.fault := core.io.fault
107	    io.wfi := core.io.wfi
108	    core.io.irq := irq_reg || dm.io.haltreq(0)
109	    core.io.timer_irq := timer_irq_reg
110	    core.io.software_irq := software_irq_reg
111	    csr.io.halted := core.io.halted
112	    csr.io.fault := core.io.fault
113	    csr.io.coralnpu_csr := core.io.csr.out
114	    core.io.debug_req := true.B
115	    core.io.csr.in.value(0) := csr.io.pcStart
116	    for (i <- 1 until p.csrInCount) {
117	      core.io.csr.in.value(i) := 0.U
118	    }
119	    require(io.debug.isDefined == core.io.debug.isDefined, "Debug port presence mismatch between CoreAxi and Core")
120	    io.debug.zip(core.io.debug).foreach { case (ioDebug, coreDebug) => ioDebug <> coreDebug }
121	    // Tie-offs (no cache to flush)
122	    core.io.dflush.ready := true.B
123	    core.io.iflush.ready := true.B
124	
125	
126	    core.io.dm.debug_req := dm.io.haltreq(0)
127	    core.io.dm.resume_req := dm.io.resumereq(0)
128	    dm.io.resumeack(0) := !core.io.dm.debug_mode && RegNext(core.io.dm.debug_mode, false.B)
129	    dm.io.halted(0) := core.io.dm.debug_mode
130	    dm.io.running(0) := !core.io.dm.debug_mode
131	    dm.io.havereset(0) := false.B
132	    core.io.dm.csr := dm.io.csr
133	    core.io.dm.csr_rs1 := dm.io.csr_rs1
134	    dm.io.csr_rd := core.io.dm.csr_rd
135	    dm.io.scalar_rd <> core.io.dm.scalar_rd
136	    dm.io.scalar_rs <> core.io.dm.scalar_rs
137	    if (p.enableFloat) {
138	      dm.io.float_rd.get <> core.io.dm.float_rd.get
139	      dm.io.float_rs.get <> core.io.dm.float_rs.get
140	    }
141	
142	    // TCMs are arbitrated between the core ({i|d}bus), the AXI slave interface,
143	    // and the debug module.
144	    val tcmPortCount = 3
145	
146	    // Build ITCM and connect to ibus
147	    val itcmSizeBytes: Int = 1024 * p.itcmSizeKBytes
148	    val itcmSubEntryWidth = 8
149	    val itcmWidth = p.axi2DataBits
150	    val itcmEntries = itcmSizeBytes / (itcmWidth / 8)
151	    val itcm = Module(new TCM128(itcmSizeBytes, itcmSubEntryWidth, memoryRegions(0).memStart))
152	    dontTouch(itcm.io)
153	    val itcmWrapper = Module(new SRAM(p, log2Ceil(itcmEntries)))
154	    itcm.io.addr := itcmWrapper.io.sram.address
155	    itcm.io.enable := itcmWrapper.io.sram.enable
156	    itcm.io.write := itcmWrapper.io.sram.isWrite
157	    itcm.io.wdata := itcmWrapper.io.sram.writeData
158	    itcm.io.wmask := itcmWrapper.io.sram.mask
159	    itcmWrapper.io.sram.readData := itcm.io.rdata
160	    val itcmArbiter = Module(new FabricArbiter(p, tcmPortCount))
161	    itcmArbiter.io.port <> itcmWrapper.io.fabric
162	
163	    // Mux ibus between ITCM (fast path) and AXI (fallback for ROM/SRAM/DDR)
164	    assert(memoryRegions(0).memType === MemoryRegionType.IMEM)
165	    val inItcm = memoryRegions(0).contains(core.io.ibus.addr)
166	
167	    // ITCM path: only issue read when address is in ITCM
168	    itcmArbiter.io.source(0).readDataAddr := MakeValid(
169	        core.io.ibus.valid && inItcm, core.io.ibus.addr)
170	    itcmArbiter.io.source(0).writeDataAddr :=
171	        MakeInvalid(UInt(p.axi2AddrBits.W))
172	    itcmArbiter.io.source(0).writeDataBits := 0.U
173	    itcmArbiter.io.source(0).writeDataStrb := 0.U
174	
175	    // AXI path: route instruction fetches outside ITCM through IBus2Axi
176	    val ibus2axi = IBus2Axi(p, id = 1)
177	    ibus2axi.io.ibus.valid := core.io.ibus.valid && !inItcm
178	    ibus2axi.io.ibus.addr := core.io.ibus.addr
179	
180	    // Mux results back to core.
181	    // Register inItcm to break the combinational cycle (addr -> inItcm -> rdata -> core -> addr).
182	    // This matches the 1-cycle SRAM read latency: rdata reflects the previous cycle's request source.
183	    val inItcmReg = RegNext(inItcm, true.B)
184	    core.io.ibus.rdata := Mux(inItcmReg, itcmArbiter.io.source(0).readData.bits, ibus2axi.io.ibus.rdata)
185	    core.io.ibus.ready := Mux(inItcm, inItcmReg, ibus2axi.io.ibus.ready)
186	    core.io.ibus.fault := ibus2axi.io.ibus.fault
187	
188	    // Build DTCM and connect to dbus
189	    val dtcmSizeBytes: Int = 1024 * p.dtcmSizeKBytes
190	    val dtcmWidth = p.axi2DataBits
191	    val dtcmEntries = dtcmSizeBytes / (dtcmWidth / 8)
192	    val dtcmSubEntryWidth = 8
193	    val dtcm = Module(new TCM128(dtcmSizeBytes, dtcmSubEntryWidth, memoryRegions(1).memStart))
194	    dontTouch(dtcm.io)
195	    val dtcmWrapper = Module(new SRAM(p, log2Ceil(dtcmEntries)))
196	    dtcm.io.addr := dtcmWrapper.io.sram.address
197	    dtcm.io.enable := dtcmWrapper.io.sram.enable
198	    dtcm.io.write := dtcmWrapper.io.sram.isWrite
199	    dtcm.io.wdata := dtcmWrapper.io.sram.writeData
200	    dtcm.io.wmask := dtcmWrapper.io.sram.mask
201	    dtcmWrapper.io.sram.readData := dtcm.io.rdata
202	    val dtcmArbiter = Module(new FabricArbiter(p, tcmPortCount))
203	    dtcmArbiter.io.port <> dtcmWrapper.io.fabric
204	    dtcmArbiter.io.source(0).readDataAddr := MakeValid(
205	        core.io.dbus.valid && !core.io.dbus.write, core.io.dbus.addr)
206	    dtcmArbiter.io.source(0).writeDataAddr := MakeValid(
207	        core.io.dbus.valid && core.io.dbus.write, core.io.dbus.addr)
208	    dtcmArbiter.io.source(0).writeDataBits := core.io.dbus.wdata
209	    dtcmArbiter.io.source(0).writeDataStrb := core.io.dbus.wmask
210	    core.io.dbus.rdata := dtcmArbiter.io.source(0).readData.bits
211	    core.io.dbus.ready := true.B  // Can always read/write TCM
212	
213	    // Connect TCMs and CSR into fabric
214	    val fabricMux = Module(new FabricMux(p, memoryRegions))
215	    fabricMux.io.ports(0) <> itcmArbiter.io.source(1)
216	    fabricMux.io.periBusy(0) := itcmArbiter.io.fabricBusy(1)
217	    fabricMux.io.ports(1) <> dtcmArbiter.io.source(1)
218	    fabricMux.io.periBusy(1) := dtcmArbiter.io.fabricBusy(1)
219	    fabricMux.io.ports(2) <> csr.io.fabric
220	    fabricMux.io.periBusy(2) := false.B
221	
222	    
223	      itcmArbiter.io.source(2) <> dm.io.itcm
224	      dtcmArbiter.io.source(2) <> dm.io.dtcm
225	
226	    // Create AXI Slave interface and connect internal fabric to AXI
227	    val axiSlave = Module(new AxiSlave(p))
228	    val axiSlaveEnable = RegInit(false.B)
229	    axiSlaveEnable := true.B
230	    axiSlave.io.fabric <> fabricMux.io.source
231	    axiSlave.io.periBusy := fabricMux.io.fabricBusy
232	    axiSlave.io.axi.write.addr <> GateDecoupled(io.axi_slave.write.addr, axiSlaveEnable)
233	    axiSlave.io.axi.write.data <> GateDecoupled(io.axi_slave.write.data, axiSlaveEnable)
234	    io.axi_slave.write.resp <> GateDecoupled(axiSlave.io.axi.write.resp, axiSlaveEnable)
235	    axiSlave.io.axi.read.addr <> GateDecoupled(io.axi_slave.read.addr, axiSlaveEnable)
236	    io.axi_slave.read.data <> GateDecoupled(axiSlave.io.axi.read.data, axiSlaveEnable)
237	
238	    // Connect ebus to AXI Master
239	    val ebus2axi = DBus2Axi(p, id = 0)
240	    ebus2axi.io.dbus <> core.io.ebus.dbus
241	    ebus2axi.io.fault <> core.io.ebus.fault
242	
243	    // AXI Arbitration
244	    // Write channel: only ebus uses it.
245	    io.axi_master.write <> ebus2axi.io.axi.write
246	
247	    // Read channel: arbitrate between ibus and ebus.
248	    val readAddrArb = Module(new CoralNPURRArbiter(new AxiAddress(p.axi2AddrBits, p.axi2DataBits, p.axi2IdBits), 2))
249	    readAddrArb.io.in(0) <> ebus2axi.io.axi.read.addr
250	    readAddrArb.io.in(1) <> ibus2axi.io.axi.addr
251	    io.axi_master.read.addr <> readAddrArb.io.out
252	
253	    // Route read data back based on ID.
254	    val readDataSkid = Queue(io.axi_master.read.data, 2)
255	    readDataSkid.ready := Mux(readDataSkid.bits.id === 1.U, ibus2axi.io.axi.data.ready, ebus2axi.io.axi.read.data.ready)
256	    ebus2axi.io.axi.read.data.valid := readDataSkid.valid && readDataSkid.bits.id === 0.U
257	    ibus2axi.io.axi.data.valid := readDataSkid.valid && readDataSkid.bits.id === 1.U
258	
259	    ebus2axi.io.axi.read.data.bits := readDataSkid.bits
260	    ibus2axi.io.axi.data.bits := readDataSkid.bits
261	  }
262	}
263

// Copyright 2024 Google LLC

// you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
// You may obtain a copy of the License at
//
//     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
//
// Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
// distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
// WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
// limitations under the License.

package coralnpu

import chisel3._
import chisel3.util._

import bus._
import common._

class CoreAxi(p: Parameters, coreModuleName: String) extends RawModule {
  override val desiredName = coreModuleName + "Axi"
  val memoryRegions = p.m
  val io = IO(new Bundle {
    // AXI
    val aclk = Input(Clock())
    val aresetn = Input(AsyncReset())
    // ITCM, DTCM, CSR
    val axi_slave = Flipped(new AxiMasterIO(p.axi2AddrBits, p.axi2DataBits, p.axi2IdBits))
    val axi_master = new AxiMasterIO(p.axi2AddrBits, p.axi2DataBits, p.axi2IdBits)
    // Core status interrupts
    val halted = Output(Bool())
    val fault = Output(Bool())
    val wfi = Output(Bool())
    val irq = Input(Bool())
    // Boot address (loaded into pcStartReg on reset)
    val boot_addr = Input(UInt(p.fetchAddrBits.W))
    val timer_irq = Input(Bool())
    val software_irq = Input(Bool())
    // Debug data interface
    val debug = Option.when(p.shouldExposeDebugPorts)(new DebugIO(p))
    val dm = new DebugModuleIO(p)
    val te = Input(Bool())
  })
  dontTouch(io)

  val rst_sync = Module(new RstSync())
  rst_sync.io.clk_i := io.aclk
  rst_sync.io.rstn_i := io.aresetn
  rst_sync.io.clk_en := true.B
  rst_sync.io.te := io.te

  val global_reset = (!Mux(io.te, io.aresetn, rst_sync.io.rstn_o).asBool).asAsyncReset
  withClockAndReset(rst_sync.io.clk_o, global_reset) {
    // Build CSR
    val csr = Module(new CoreCSR(p))
    csr.io.internal := false.B
    csr.io.bootAddr := io.boot_addr

    // Build core and connect with CSR
    val cg = Module(new ClockGate)
    cg.io.clk_i := rst_sync.io.clk_o
    cg.io.te := io.te

    val dm = Module(new DebugModule(p))
        dontTouch(dm.io)
    val dmEnable = RegInit(false.B)
    dmEnable := true.B
    val dmReqArbiter = Module(new CoralNPURRArbiter(new DebugModuleReqIO(p), 2))
    dmReqArbiter.io.in(0) <> GateDecoupled(io.dm.req, dmEnable)
    dmReqArbiter.io.in(1) <> GateDecoupled(csr.io.debug.req, dmEnable)

    // Queue source ID of the request to route the response later.
    val inflight = Module(new Queue(UInt(1.W), 1))

    dm.io.ext.req.bits := dmReqArbiter.io.out.bits
    dm.io.ext.req.valid := dmReqArbiter.io.out.valid && inflight.io.enq.ready
    dmReqArbiter.io.out.ready := dm.io.ext.req.ready && inflight.io.enq.ready

    inflight.io.enq.bits := dmReqArbiter.io.chosen
    inflight.io.enq.valid := dmReqArbiter.io.out.valid && dm.io.ext.req.ready

    val rspId = inflight.io.deq.bits
    inflight.io.deq.ready := dm.io.ext.rsp.fire

    csr.io.debug.rsp.bits := dm.io.ext.rsp.bits
    io.dm.rsp.bits := dm.io.ext.rsp.bits

    csr.io.debug.rsp.valid := dm.io.ext.rsp.valid && inflight.io.deq.valid && (rspId === 1.U)
    io.dm.rsp.valid := dm.io.ext.rsp.valid && inflight.io.deq.valid && (rspId === 0.U)

    dm.io.ext.rsp.ready := inflight.io.deq.valid && Mux(rspId === 1.U, csr.io.debug.rsp.ready, io.dm.rsp.ready)

    val core_reset = Mux(io.te, (!io.aresetn.asBool).asAsyncReset, (csr.io.reset || dm.io.ndmreset).asAsyncReset)
    val core = withClockAndReset(cg.io.clk_o, core_reset) { Core(p, coreModuleName) }

    // Register interrupts at the boundary to break timing paths to ibus.
    val irq_reg = RegNext(io.irq, false.B)
    val timer_irq_reg = RegNext(io.timer_irq, false.B)
    val software_irq_reg = RegNext(io.software_irq, false.B)

    cg.io.enable := irq_reg || timer_irq_reg || software_irq_reg || (!csr.io.cg && !core.io.wfi) || dm.io.haltreq(0)
    io.halted := core.io.halted
    io.fault := core.io.fault
    io.wfi := core.io.wfi
    core.io.irq := irq_reg || dm.io.haltreq(0)
    core.io.timer_irq := timer_irq_reg
    core.io.software_irq := software_irq_reg
    csr.io.halted := core.io.halted
    csr.io.fault := core.io.fault
    csr.io.coralnpu_csr := core.io.csr.out
    core.io.debug_req := true.B
    core.io.csr.in.value(0) := csr.io.pcStart
    for (i <- 1 until p.csrInCount) {
      core.io.csr.in.value(i) := 0.U
    }
    require(io.debug.isDefined == core.io.debug.isDefined, "Debug port presence mismatch between CoreAxi and Core")
    io.debug.zip(core.io.debug).foreach { case (ioDebug, coreDebug) => ioDebug <> coreDebug }
    // Tie-offs (no cache to flush)
    core.io.dflush.ready := true.B
    core.io.iflush.ready := true.B


    core.io.dm.debug_req := dm.io.haltreq(0)
    core.io.dm.resume_req := dm.io.resumereq(0)
    dm.io.resumeack(0) := !core.io.dm.debug_mode && RegNext(core.io.dm.debug_mode, false.B)
    dm.io.halted(0) := core.io.dm.debug_mode
    dm.io.running(0) := !core.io.dm.debug_mode
    dm.io.havereset(0) := false.B
    core.io.dm.csr := dm.io.csr
    core.io.dm.csr_rs1 := dm.io.csr_rs1
    dm.io.csr_rd := core.io.dm.csr_rd
    dm.io.scalar_rd <> core.io.dm.scalar_rd
    dm.io.scalar_rs <> core.io.dm.scalar_rs
    if (p.enableFloat) {
      dm.io.float_rd.get <> core.io.dm.float_rd.get
      dm.io.float_rs.get <> core.io.dm.float_rs.get
    }

    // TCMs are arbitrated between the core ({i|d}bus), the AXI slave interface,
    // and the debug module.
    val tcmPortCount = 3

    // Build ITCM and connect to ibus
    val itcmSizeBytes: Int = 1024 * p.itcmSizeKBytes
    val itcmSubEntryWidth = 8
    val itcmWidth = p.axi2DataBits
    val itcmEntries = itcmSizeBytes / (itcmWidth / 8)
    val itcm = Module(new TCM128(itcmSizeBytes, itcmSubEntryWidth, memoryRegions(0).memStart))
    dontTouch(itcm.io)
    val itcmWrapper = Module(new SRAM(p, log2Ceil(itcmEntries)))
    itcm.io.addr := itcmWrapper.io.sram.address
    itcm.io.enable := itcmWrapper.io.sram.enable
    itcm.io.write := itcmWrapper.io.sram.isWrite
    itcm.io.wdata := itcmWrapper.io.sram.writeData
    itcm.io.wmask := itcmWrapper.io.sram.mask
    itcmWrapper.io.sram.readData := itcm.io.rdata
    val itcmArbiter = Module(new FabricArbiter(p, tcmPortCount))
    itcmArbiter.io.port <> itcmWrapper.io.fabric

    // Mux ibus between ITCM (fast path) and AXI (fallback for ROM/SRAM/DDR)
    assert(memoryRegions(0).memType === MemoryRegionType.IMEM)
    val inItcm = memoryRegions(0).contains(core.io.ibus.addr)

    // ITCM path: only issue read when address is in ITCM
    itcmArbiter.io.source(0).readDataAddr := MakeValid(
        core.io.ibus.valid && inItcm, core.io.ibus.addr)
    itcmArbiter.io.source(0).writeDataAddr :=
        MakeInvalid(UInt(p.axi2AddrBits.W))
    itcmArbiter.io.source(0).writeDataBits := 0.U
    itcmArbiter.io.source(0).writeDataStrb := 0.U

    // AXI path: route instruction fetches outside ITCM through IBus2Axi
    val ibus2axi = IBus2Axi(p, id = 1)
    ibus2axi.io.ibus.valid := core.io.ibus.valid && !inItcm
    ibus2axi.io.ibus.addr := core.io.ibus.addr

    // Mux results back to core.
    // Register inItcm to break the combinational cycle (addr -> inItcm -> rdata -> core -> addr).
    // This matches the 1-cycle SRAM read latency: rdata reflects the previous cycle's request source.
    val inItcmReg = RegNext(inItcm, true.B)
    core.io.ibus.rdata := Mux(inItcmReg, itcmArbiter.io.source(0).readData.bits, ibus2axi.io.ibus.rdata)
    core.io.ibus.ready := Mux(inItcm, inItcmReg, ibus2axi.io.ibus.ready)
    core.io.ibus.fault := ibus2axi.io.ibus.fault

    // Build DTCM and connect to dbus
    val dtcmSizeBytes: Int = 1024 * p.dtcmSizeKBytes
    val dtcmWidth = p.axi2DataBits
    val dtcmEntries = dtcmSizeBytes / (dtcmWidth / 8)
    val dtcmSubEntryWidth = 8
    val dtcm = Module(new TCM128(dtcmSizeBytes, dtcmSubEntryWidth, memoryRegions(1).memStart))
    dontTouch(dtcm.io)
    val dtcmWrapper = Module(new SRAM(p, log2Ceil(dtcmEntries)))
    dtcm.io.addr := dtcmWrapper.io.sram.address
    dtcm.io.enable := dtcmWrapper.io.sram.enable
    dtcm.io.write := dtcmWrapper.io.sram.isWrite
    dtcm.io.wdata := dtcmWrapper.io.sram.writeData
    dtcm.io.wmask := dtcmWrapper.io.sram.mask
    dtcmWrapper.io.sram.readData := dtcm.io.rdata
    val dtcmArbiter = Module(new FabricArbiter(p, tcmPortCount))
    dtcmArbiter.io.port <> dtcmWrapper.io.fabric
    dtcmArbiter.io.source(0).readDataAddr := MakeValid(
        core.io.dbus.valid && !core.io.dbus.write, core.io.dbus.addr)
    dtcmArbiter.io.source(0).writeDataAddr := MakeValid(
        core.io.dbus.valid && core.io.dbus.write, core.io.dbus.addr)
    dtcmArbiter.io.source(0).writeDataBits := core.io.dbus.wdata
    dtcmArbiter.io.source(0).writeDataStrb := core.io.dbus.wmask
    core.io.dbus.rdata := dtcmArbiter.io.source(0).readData.bits
    core.io.dbus.ready := true.B  // Can always read/write TCM

    // Connect TCMs and CSR into fabric
    val fabricMux = Module(new FabricMux(p, memoryRegions))
    fabricMux.io.ports(0) <> itcmArbiter.io.source(1)
    fabricMux.io.periBusy(0) := itcmArbiter.io.fabricBusy(1)
    fabricMux.io.ports(1) <> dtcmArbiter.io.source(1)
    fabricMux.io.periBusy(1) := dtcmArbiter.io.fabricBusy(1)
    fabricMux.io.ports(2) <> csr.io.fabric
    fabricMux.io.periBusy(2) := false.B

    
      itcmArbiter.io.source(2) <> dm.io.itcm
      dtcmArbiter.io.source(2) <> dm.io.dtcm

    // Create AXI Slave interface and connect internal fabric to AXI
    val axiSlave = Module(new AxiSlave(p))
    val axiSlaveEnable = RegInit(false.B)
    axiSlaveEnable := true.B
    axiSlave.io.fabric <> fabricMux.io.source
    axiSlave.io.periBusy := fabricMux.io.fabricBusy
    axiSlave.io.axi.write.addr <> GateDecoupled(io.axi_slave.write.addr, axiSlaveEnable)
    axiSlave.io.axi.write.data <> GateDecoupled(io.axi_slave.write.data, axiSlaveEnable)
    io.axi_slave.write.resp <> GateDecoupled(axiSlave.io.axi.write.resp, axiSlaveEnable)
    axiSlave.io.axi.read.addr <> GateDecoupled(io.axi_slave.read.addr, axiSlaveEnable)
    io.axi_slave.read.data <> GateDecoupled(axiSlave.io.axi.read.data, axiSlaveEnable)

    // Connect ebus to AXI Master
    val ebus2axi = DBus2Axi(p, id = 0)
    ebus2axi.io.dbus <> core.io.ebus.dbus
    ebus2axi.io.fault <> core.io.ebus.fault

    // AXI Arbitration
    // Write channel: only ebus uses it.
    io.axi_master.write <> ebus2axi.io.axi.write

    // Read channel: arbitrate between ibus and ebus.
    val readAddrArb = Module(new CoralNPURRArbiter(new AxiAddress(p.axi2AddrBits, p.axi2DataBits, p.axi2IdBits), 2))
    readAddrArb.io.in(0) <> ebus2axi.io.axi.read.addr
    readAddrArb.io.in(1) <> ibus2axi.io.axi.addr
    io.axi_master.read.addr <> readAddrArb.io.out

    // Route read data back based on ID.
    val readDataSkid = Queue(io.axi_master.read.data, 2)
    readDataSkid.ready := Mux(readDataSkid.bits.id === 1.U, ibus2axi.io.axi.data.ready, ebus2axi.io.axi.read.data.ready)
    ebus2axi.io.axi.read.data.valid := readDataSkid.valid && readDataSkid.bits.id === 0.U
    ibus2axi.io.axi.data.valid := readDataSkid.valid && readDataSkid.bits.id === 1.U

    ebus2axi.io.axi.read.data.bits := readDataSkid.bits
    ibus2axi.io.axi.data.bits := readDataSkid.bits
  }
}

1	// Copyright 2024 Google LLC
2	//
3	// Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
4	// you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
5	// You may obtain a copy of the License at
